sections.paragraph:
ἀσεβής, ές acc. sing. ἀσεβῆν Ro 4:5 v.l., B-D-F §46, 1 (s. prec. entry; Aeschyl., Thu.+) pert. to violating norms for a proper relation to deity, irreverent, impious, ungodly.

ⓐ of pers., ἁμαρτωλὸς ἀ. Jd 15 (En 1:9). οἱ ἀ. ἄνθρωποι (UPZ 162 III, 8 [117 b.c.] ὑπὸ ἀσεβῶν ἀνθρώπων) 2 Pt 3:7. Mostly subst. ὁ ἀ. impious (person) (Diod S 1, 96, 5; 3, 61, 5; 5, 71, 2 and 6; OGI 90, 23; 26; LXX; TestZeb 10:3) Ro 5:6; 2 Pt 2:5; 2:6; Jd 4, 15; 1 Cl 14:5 (Ps 36:35); 18:13 (Ps 50:15); 57:7 (Pr 1:32); B 10:10 (Ps 1:1); 11:7 (Ps 1:4ff); (w. ἁμαρτωλός) 1 Ti 1:9; (w. ἄνομος) B 15:5; Dg 9:4; (w. κεκριμένοι τῷ θανάτῳ) B 10:5; in contrast to those who serve God and in the fear of God are eager to live uprightly οἱ κρινόμενοι ἀ. the ungodly who are (already) condemned 2 Cl 18:1. Punished w. eternal fire MPol 11:2 (Diod S 4, 74, 2 the ἀσεβεῖς in everlasting torment in a subterranean place of punishment).—The collective sg. (as Lucian, Bacch. 7 ὁ γέρων = οἱ γέροντες; Ps.-Dicaearch. p. 146 F. πολὺς ὁ καταπλέων ἐστίν; EpArist 13) Ro 4:5; 1 Pt 4:18 (Pr 11:31).—ÅStröm, Vetekornet, Studier över individ och kollektivi NT ’44.

ⓑ of human characteristics (w. ἄδικος, as Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 90; Jos., Ant. 8, 251; cp. ἀσεβέσιν ὁδοῖς ApcSed 15:5) ζῆλος ἀ. 1 Cl 3:4. Impers. (Epict. 4, 7, 11; SIG 204, 52 ὅπως ἂν μηδὲν ἀσεβὲς γίγνηται) πῶς οὐκ ἀσεβές; how is it not impious? Dg 4:3. V.l. for ἀσθενής 1 Cl 59:4 (vide).—M-M. TW.

acc. acc. = accusative

sing. sing. = singular

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

prec. prec. = preceding

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

pers. pers. = person(s)

En En = I Enoch—List 2

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

ff ff = (and) following

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. sg. = singular

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

NT NT = New Testament

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 141
